chapter.title: Raster Sampling
chapter.para: The PostGIS raster extension stores gridded coverage data (elevation, temperature, satellite imagery, …) in the raster type. Each raster has one or more bands; a band holds a 2D array of pixel values, a spatial extent, a pixel size, and an optional nodata sentinel value.

MobilityDB's raster sampling operator evaluates a raster band at the instants of a moving-point trajectory and returns a tfloat. Instants whose position falls outside the raster extent or on a nodata pixel are silently dropped. This enables queries such as what elevation profile did each vehicle follow? or which trips entered a temperature threshold band?

To use the operators described here, MobilityDB must be built with -DRASTER=ON. On such a build the generated mobilitydb.control declares requires = 'postgis, postgis_raster', so a single CASCADE creates the full stack:

listitem.para: Sample a raster band at each instant of a trajectory

Evaluates pixel band at every instant of traj using bilinear-nearest sampling (ST_Value with resample=false). Returns an instant-set tfloat whose values are rounded to four decimal places. Returns NULL when no instant intersects the raster.
Applied to the BerlinMOD trajectory dataset with a terrain elevation raster:

sect1.title: Build and Install
sect1.para: PostGIS raster is part of the standard postgresql-N-postgis-3 package on Debian/Ubuntu; no extra package is required. Pass -DRASTER=ON to CMake:
To include raster in the CI coverage build alongside other optional families, add -DRASTER=ON to the cmake invocation in .github/workflows/pgversion.yml.

sect1.title: Production Roadmap
sect1.para: The operator in this chapter provides the core SQL surface. A production-grade integration would extend it with:

listitem.para: MEOS kernel function — a C-level raster_value(raster, tgeompoint) that returns a heap-allocated Temporal *, enabling PyMEOS, JMEOS, and other bindings to auto-derive the operator without a PostgreSQL round-trip.
Raster table integration — a set-returning variant that joins against a tiled raster coverage (raster_value(raster[], tgeompoint)) and merges tile results.
Band-aware tfloat array — return one tfloat per band for multi-spectral rasters.
Inverse operator — raster_atTime(raster, tstzspan) restricts a raster to the spatial extent visited by one or more trajectories during a time interval.
